catalysts.thesis: Rhythm Pharmaceuticals is approaching a major FDA PDUFA date for setmelanotide in acquired hypothalamic obesity (HO), a label expansion that could essentially double its total addressable market.
catalysts.catalyst_details: The FDA has set a PDUFA goal date of March 20, 2026, for the sNDA in HO. Expanded Phase 3 TRANSCEND data was recently presented (March 1, 2026) showcasing an 18.8% placebo-adjusted BMI reduction, significantly de-risking the regulatory decision.
catalysts.absorption_status: The market has not fully priced in the binary upside of the PDUFA date. Despite a clean Q4 earnings beat, the stock remains stable (+2.25% recently) without a massive pre-approval run-up, avoiding the 8% overbought threshold.
catalysts.prediction_market: 85% implied probability of FDA approval based on Phase 3 data strength.
catalysts.recency_proof: March 8, 2026 - PDUFA Countdown and Phase 3 update coverage [1.14].
catalysts.risk: The FDA could issue a Complete Response Letter (CRL) citing manufacturing bottlenecks or restrictive labeling, despite the strong clinical efficacy.
catalysts.mispricing_evidence: Options markets are underpricing the magnitude of the TAM expansion, treating the event as a standard label update rather than a secondary monopoly in a high-unmet-need condition.

catalysts.thesis: A massive, multi-million dollar insider buy by a high-ranking executive signals peak confidence in the underlying portfolio of this beaten-down specialty lending stock.
catalysts.catalyst_details: Vice President Alan Waxman aggressively purchased 300,000 shares of common stock for $5.53 million (at prices between $18.42 and $18.46) on March 5-6, 2026, which was publicly disclosed via SEC Form 4 on March 9-10, 2026.
catalysts.absorption_status: Completely unpriced. The stock currently trades around $18.39, reflecting a 22% 6-month decline, and has not yet spiked in response to the newly filed insider data.
catalysts.recency_proof: March 10, 2026 - Investing.com Monday Insider Activity Report.
catalysts.risk: Broader macroeconomic pressure on middle-market corporate borrowers could drive an increase in non-accrual loans.
catalysts.mispricing_evidence: A single insider dropping $5.5M in open-market buys near 52-week lows is a high-conviction statistical anomaly for a $1.5B market cap BDC, suggesting extreme discount to net asset value (NAV).

catalysts.thesis: Aggressive insider buying into a multi-month momentum rally demonstrates that management views the stock as fundamentally undervalued relative to its infrastructure backlog.
catalysts.catalyst_details: On March 9, 2026, a Director at Tutor Perini Corp disclosed a $1.82 million stock purchase, marking their second-largest buy ever. This follows another insider's purchase in the prior month.
catalysts.absorption_status: While the stock has strong yearly momentum, the market has not yet gap-traded the new, large-scale $1.82M Form 4 filing disclosed on March 9.
catalysts.recency_proof: March 9, 2026 - Stocks Insiders are Buying Report.
catalysts.risk: Infrastructure project delays, municipal budget bottlenecks, or unforeseen material cost overruns could erode forward margins.
catalysts.mispricing_evidence: Insiders typically trim positions or sell into 100%+ run-ups. Heavy cluster buying at current levels strongly suggests the current forward P/E is too cheap given newly awarded contracts.
